issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .features-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background (#ffffff). At 18px font size (large text), this provides approximately 2.2:1 contrast ratio, failing the 3:1 requirement for large text under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 819: `.features-section .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which renders no visible focus ring because outline-width is zero. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement is provided in this rule or a paired base rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. More critically, this implementation does not remove content from the accessibility tree correctly and may cause layout issues while remaining visible, defeating the purpose of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382-393: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding, creating a 28px × 28px touch target. This is below the 44px recommended size and offers less than the ideal minimum, potentially causing activation difficulties for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, creating a touch target of only 32px. While this meets the absolute minimum of 24px, it falls below the 44px recommended by WCAG 2.5.8 for comfortable touch activation, and the vertical padding (10px) does not extend the target size beyond the min-height in border-box mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), allowing spatial movement animations. There is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: (add after line 696) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px with only 6px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target below the 44px recommended size. The padding is also less than 8px, providing insufficient compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, creating a 20px × 20px touch target. This is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum), making it difficult for users with limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no additional padding, resulting in a touch target of exactly 24px. While this meets the absolute minimum, it falls significantly below the 44px recommended size for comfortable activation without adjacent spacing compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1374: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), enabling spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or override this transform transition for users who require reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: (add after line 437) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }` or specifically target the transform: `transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast);` (removing transform)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class animates transform property (transition: transform var(--transition-base)), creating spatial movement when the link appears. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule exists to disable this transition for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: (add after line 425) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: translateY(-1px), creating spatial movement. While the transition makes it animated, the transform itself is a spatial change that should be suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion to avoid triggering vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: (add after line 698) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), creating spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 1088 only covers .pricing-card:hover, not this specific .pricing-card--featured:hover selector, leaving the animation active for users requesting reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1094: (add after line 1094) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The active state for buttons applies transform: translateY(1px), creating spatial movement when the button is pressed. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform for users who require reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: (add after line 441) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform to animate in from off-screen, which is spatial movement that should be disabled for users who prefer reduced motion to avoid vestibular issues.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring in standard display modes. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The forced-colors media query only helps high-contrast mode users, leaving standard users without any indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: Replace with `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state explicitly removes the outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: Replace with `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility modifier changes the outline offset but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. It does not restore a visible outline style, leaving interactive elements using this class without any focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 542: Replace with `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies a translateY transform on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion, which can cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 527: Add after existing rules: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum for touch targets. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may have difficulty activating these buttons without accidentally hitting adjacent elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: Change `min-height: 32px;` to `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility sets only outline-color while the global outline: none remains in effect. Without an outline-style and outline-width, no visible focus ring appears, causing keyboard users to lose track of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 546: Replace with `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px, which falls between the 24px minimum and 44px recommended size. This may be too small for reliable activation by users with limited dexterity or on small touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: Change `min-height: 36px;` to `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, well below the 44px recommended touch target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor disabilities to activate the search function reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766: Change `width: 28px;` to `width: 44px;` and Line 767: Change `height: 28px;` to `height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has a fixed width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: Change `width: 20px; height: 20px; min-height: 20px;` to `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a 24px

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Users in high-contrast mode rely on visible focus indicators and will lose track of focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negative spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision, as words may visually run together.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the base rule. Setting color on a zero-width outline produces no visible focus ring, failing to indicate focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible overflow, which renders the content visually instead of hiding it. Conversely, it fails to use absolute positioning and clipping to hide content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users may receive duplicated or incorrectly positioned content.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search button has an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. While this meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes the touch target too small for users with motor disabilities to reliably activate.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition, causing spatial movement on interaction. No reduced motion media query disables this effect, which may trigger vestibular symptoms in sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px with min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector removes text underlines without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. This causes links to be distinguishable from surrounding text only by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. This leaves no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the submenu, making it impossible to track focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement visual indicator. Keyboard users will not see when this button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible with no visible alternative. This prevents keyboard users from seeing which link is currently focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes a transform transition that creates spatial movement on hover. The empty prefers-reduced-motion block at line 698 fails to override this animation, potentially causing discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing to -0.05em, compressing character spacing. This reduces readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities who rely on clear character separation.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 elements use var(--letter-spacing-tight) which evaluates to -0.025em, compressing text horizontally. This can reduce readability for users with low vision or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule suppresses the outline and provides only an outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard. This prevents users from knowing which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, meeting the minimum but below the 44px recommended size, with no padding specified to increase the interactive area. This creates a small touch target that may be difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, a negative value that reduces the space between words. This can make text significantly harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by causing words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 133:   word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline in the focus-visible state without providing a replacement visual indicator such as a box-shadow or border change. This makes the button invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues for users with vestibular disorders. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button difficult or impossible for some users to activate with touch or pointer devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640:   min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor element selector removes text underlines without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as a border-bottom or font-weight change. This causes links to rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 161: a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the focus outline with outline: none and provide only a negative outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which menu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button suppresses the focus outline with only an outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without any visual indication of focus when navigating to the button.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which link is currently focused.

issuesFound.title: Search input focus-visible indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the focus outline in the :focus-visible state without providing a replacement, even though the :focus state has a box-shadow. This inconsistency means keyboard users relying on :focus-visible (the standard for keyboard navigation) see no focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Icon-only buttons are particularly critical touch targets and must meet the minimum size requirement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 655:   min-width: 24px;
  Line 656:   min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but falling below the 44px recommended target size, and has zero padding to compensate. This may cause difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 877:   width: 44px;
  Line 878:   height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Logo link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link removes the text underline without providing an alternative visual indicator such as a border-bottom or background highlight. If the link colour is not sufficiently distinct from surrounding text, users cannot identify it as a link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680:   border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links remove text underlines and rely solely on colour changes to indicate interactivity. Without an underline, border-bottom, or other non-colour visual differentiator in the default state, users may not recognise these as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750:   border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove text underlines without providing a non-colour visual differentiator in the default state. Combined with the likely subtle colour difference in the footer, this makes links indistinguishable from static text for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset but lacks a visible focus ring (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). Keyboard users will not see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Users navigating via keyboard cannot identify the mobile menu toggle when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 803: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have outline: none on focus-visible with no visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within submenus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the button focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and lacks compensating padding. Touch targets should be at least 24px, preferably 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 885: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visually visible instead of hidden. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality, exposing internal labels to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition and transform for hover/active states without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the button movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

Line 609: .skip-link { position: fixed; top: var(--space-3); left: var(--space-3); z-index: var(--z-toast); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); border: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); transform: none; transition: none; white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.fix: Line 370: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum threshold for touch targets. This makes the button difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons require larger touch targets for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px with no padding compensation. This is below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized to 20px by 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes them difficult to select for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content meant for screen readers only will be visible on screen.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 403: .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. The global prefers-reduced-motion media query does not override this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform and transition for a 3D effect. The prefers-reduced-motion media query block for this selector is empty.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a replacement, removing focus indication for high-contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The default button has min-height: 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended target size for ease of use by users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not properly hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. It should use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode :focus-visible focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector repeats the same anti-pattern (outline: none with only outline-offset), removing the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users. No visible alternative is provided in the same rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline as a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em (negative value), which reduces the space between words. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px with no visible alternative (box-shadow or border) in the same rule. The link becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text remains visible on screen, breaking the layout and potentially confusing sighted users, while the intended functionality (accessible only to AT) is broken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 478-491: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img applies a 3D transform (rotateY, rotateX) with a transition, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (lines 1165-1170) is empty and does not disable the transform. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the parallax/rotation effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165-1170: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Search form label hidden from assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search label.sr-only applies display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users cannot perceive the purpose of the search input, as the label is hidden from both sight and assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 931: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines (text-decoration: none) without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) in the static state. Links rely solely on colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; } (or alternatively) Line 237: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The base :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. outline-offset does not create a visible ring if outline width is 0. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus state on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus state removes outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the home link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes outline on focus without visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove outline on focus without box-shadow or border replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove outline on focus without visible alternative.

issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes outline on focus without visible alternative.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on focus without visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, making content visible instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform transition but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 403 is empty and does not disable it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transition but is not covered by a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform transition but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 713 is empty.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em, which reduces space between letters and impairs readability.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error message ID to aria-describedby but neglects to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes to display the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2158: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Assistive technologies will report the button as collapsed even though the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2228: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing.highlightPlan() appends to aria-label repeatedly
issuesFound.explanation: Each time a plan is highlighted, the function appends " (Recommended)" to the existing aria-label instead of replacing it. Repeated calls cause the accessible name to grow indefinitely (e.g., "Pro (Recommended) (Recommended) (Recommended)").
issuesFound.fix: Line 3756: Replace the string concatenation with a template that includes the original text without appending: `plan.el.setAttribute('aria-label', \`\${plan.el.textContent.trim()} \${STRINGS.recommendedPlan || '(Recommended)'}\`);` or store the original label separately.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function animates the panel open and updates CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the accordion item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3425: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the function does not move keyboard focus into the drawer. Focus remains on the trigger button, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page content behind the drawer to reach the navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2168: After `_lockScroll();`, add `_drawer.focus({ preventScroll: true });` or focus the first focusable child within the drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds classes and removes the hidden attribute to show the navigation drawer, and announces the state change via liveRegion, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry that leaves screen readers with incorrect state information (false) when the menu is actually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1708: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false". This inconsistency means assistive technologies will not be informed when the dropdown opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1889: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function updates the item state, animates the panel, and removes the hidden attribute, but it omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state correctly when a user opens an FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2810: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. The corresponding markValid function removes aria-invalid. Without aria-invalid="true", assistive technologies may not identify the field as being in an error state, relying solely on the description which might be missed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1438: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic number updates not announced via aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates the textContent of the statistic element repeatedly during the animation sequence to count up to the target value. However, the element is not given an aria-live attribute. Screen reader users will not be notified of the changing numbers or the final value unless they manually refocus the element after the animation completes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3618: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

fpTitles: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus into drawer
dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
statCounterAnimator dynamic number updates not announced via aria-live

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1282: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function updates CSS classes and emits events but never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot perceive that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1935: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2152: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion panel is expanded via animateHeight and the hidden attribute is removed, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" when opening an item.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3422: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons lack aria-pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate the active category via CSS classes only; the code does not set aria-pressed (or aria-selected) to communicate the active state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable for focus management but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab outside the open navigation drawer and focus is not returned on close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (_drawer) visibility is toggled via CSS classes (is-open) in open and close functions, but aria-hidden is not updated on the drawer element itself. When visually hidden, the drawer should be removed from the accessibility tree to prevent screen reader users from navigating invisible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.open, add: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. In mobileNav.close, add: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element (inst.trigger). The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing state mismatch for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In dropdownMenu._openInstance function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, the state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that a menu has appeared or disappeared, unlike other components like mobileNav or faqAccordion which use liveRegion.announce.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In dropdownMenu._openInstance, add: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');. In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, add: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function updates the item state and reveals the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving screen reader users unaware that the answer has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In faqAccordion.open function, add: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field itself. This prevents assistive technologies from explicitly identifying the field as invalid, relying solely on the description link.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In srUtils.markInvalid function, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having a validation error.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands an FAQ answer panel and updates visual state but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not convey that the answer is now visible.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users will not be informed that the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology will not be informed that the menu has closed and focus will return to the trigger.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu does not trigger a live region announcement, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has appeared, especially if focus is not immediately moved into the menu.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle active visual states but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state to screen reader users, violating the toggle button pattern requirements.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the menu has closed, particularly if focus remains on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the function emits a custom event but does not announce the collapsed state via the live region. Screen reader users will not be notified that the answer is now hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function declares a focus trap variable but never initializes or activates it when opening the navigation drawer. Keyboard focus will not be constrained within the open menu, allowing users to tab outside the drawer into the background page.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving screen reader users unaware the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state to open and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", resulting in incorrect accessibility state reporting for accordion items.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the field as invalid when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed when a menu opens or closes dynamically.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the drawer using CSS classes but do not update aria-hidden on the drawer element itself. This may leave the drawer accessible to screen readers when visually hidden or vice versa.

issuesFound.explanation: The function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the function;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer and emits a custom event, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after _open = true;

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates price display elements and emits a custom event, but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Switched to annual billing") to screen readers. Users are not notified that prices have changed on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing'); (or monthly equivalent) after updating the display;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technology with stale state information indicating the menu is still open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when the menu closes;

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products and updating the visible card count, the function emits a custom event but does not announce the result count (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers. Users are unaware that the filter has been applied or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2600: Add liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`); after _updateCountBadge;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function emits a custom event but does not announce the expanded state or the question title to screen readers. Users are not notified that content has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); after opening the panel;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid removal
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Valid fields may still be reported as invalid by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); inside the function;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function declares a focus trap (_trap) but never calls _trap.activate() when opening the drawer. Keyboard focus is not confined to the navigation menu, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: Add if (_trap) _trap.activate(); after opening the drawer;

issuesFound.title: createRovingTabindex missing aria-orientation

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

The `close` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the answer expands.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in `srUtils` links the error message via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to immediately announce that a field has an error state upon focus.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` identifies the `_trigger` element but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. The `open` and `close` functions also fail to toggle this attribute on the trigger, leaving the button's state unknown to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in `pricing` updates the visible prices and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the pricing context has changed.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function in `productFilter` updates the visual count badge via `_updateCountBadge` but does not announce the new result count (e.g., "5 products found") to the live region. Screen reader users filtering products receive no feedback on the number of results.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` updates `textContent` of the stat element to show the counted number. Since the element lacks `aria-live` and no separate live region is updated, the changing numbers are not announced to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onChipClick` function in `activeFilterChips` removes a filter and re-renders the list, but it does not announce the action (e.g., "Filter removed") to the live region. Screen reader users are not confirmed that their request to remove a filter was processed.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent acceptance not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept` function in `cookieConsent` stores the decision and hides the banner, but it does not announce "Cookie preferences saved" or similar to the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the banner dismissal was successful.

issuesFound.title: Validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function in `srUtils` removes the `aria-describedby` reference but does not remove the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute or set it to `"false"`. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technology even after the error is resolved.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() utility links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Without this attribute, screen readers do not announce the field as invalid when users navigate to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions update CSS classes to show or hide the navigation drawer, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button (_trigger). Screen-reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1463: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the open() function.
  Line 1481: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;` inside the close() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the result count to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen are not informed when filtering completes or how many items match the selected criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the applyFilters function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() methods manage the visibility of dropdown panels but do not synchronise the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and leaves screen-reader users unaware of panel visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1620: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside _openInstance().
  Line 1635: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function dynamically updates price values and billing labels but provides no auditory feedback. Screen-reader users receive no confirmation that prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` immediately after the line `requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; });` inside the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and removes the hidden attribute, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). While the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the missing "true" value in open() leaves the state inconsistent for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on page load. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger must explicitly declare its collapsed state so screen readers report it correctly on first encounter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;` in the init() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function in productFilter toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Because these tabs act as toggle buttons (mutually exclusive selections), aria-pressed is required to convey the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() utility clears error associations but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Fields that were previously invalid remain flagged as invalid in the accessibility tree even after passing validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`) immediately after the existing logic that updates aria-describedby inside the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets the item.open state to true, but it does not set the aria-expanded attribute to "true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded to "false", the mismatch means screen readers only hear the collapsed state and never the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open function, after item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')): `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function dynamically updates all plan card prices and billing period labels when the user switches between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce the price changes or the new billing period to screen readers. Users are not informed that the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _syncToggleState(period)): `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices updated to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) function as a mutually exclusive button group where one button is always pressed, but the _syncToggleState function only updates CSS classes and does not set the aria-pressed attribute. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside _periodBtns.forEach, after toggleClass): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module manages a combobox pattern where the _open and _close functions control the visibility of the suggestion listbox, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the search input element. Screen readers cannot inform users when the suggestion dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open function, after _searchState.open = true): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line (in _close function, after _searchState.open = false): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter pagination not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The goToPage function changes the displayed page of product results and scrolls the grid into view, but it does not announce the page change or the current page number to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the product list has changed to a new page.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after scrollIntoView(_gridEl)): `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing page \${s.page} of product results\`);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter category tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons in the product filter module function as toggle buttons that remain pressed when active, but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes and does not set the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the active state. Screen readers cannot identify which filter category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside forEach loop, after toggleClass): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function identifies the trigger button (_trigger) and the drawer element (_drawer), but it does not programmatically set the aria-controls attribute on the trigger to reference the drawer's ID. Screen readers cannot identify which element is controlled by the navigation toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init function, after _drawer assignment): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-haspopup
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button opens a menu drawer, but the init function does not set the aria-haspopup attribute to indicate that the button controls a menu. Screen readers cannot identify the button as a menu opener.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init function, after setting aria-controls): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.HASPOPUP, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function mobileNav.open: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after _open = true;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function mobileNav.close: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); before _open = false;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may announce the incorrect initial state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden using CSS classes (is-open) without setting aria-hidden="true" or the hidden attribute. The content remains accessible to screen readers even when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded update on open

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded update on close
