issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open class from the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialize aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function binds event listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button if the menu is closed by default. The ARIA state may not match the visual state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765: if (_trigger && !_open) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1905: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialize aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The ARIA state may not match the visual state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1945: if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update ARIA state on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates the visual checked class on the billing toggle but fails to update aria-checked (for role="switch") or aria-pressed (for button). Screen reader users will not know the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3505: setAttr(_toggleEl, _toggleEl.getAttribute('role') === 'switch' ? 'aria-checked' : 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce count change
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product count badge but does not announce the change via liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of products changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3885: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick does not announce action
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action via liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1535: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but fails to remove or reset aria-invalid to "false". The field may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); // ... existing describedBy logic

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but no element with that ID is rendered because FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (FormGroup): <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button tracks an on/off state (isPlaying) but uses only aria-label. It should use aria-pressed to communicate the toggle state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.fix: Line 2210 (FormGroup): <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but fails to render the aria-invalid attribute on the underlying input element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the error state to users when form validation fails.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons visually indicate the active state but lack aria-pressed to communicate selection to assistive technology. Users cannot identify which category is currently active.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button does not expose the expanded state of the answer panel via aria-expanded. Screen reader users are not informed whether the FAQ answer is visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible do not have aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce all testimonials in the DOM regardless of which slide is visually active, causing confusion.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate validation errors but only applies a CSS class. It does not map the prop to the `aria-invalid` attribute, so screen readers cannot announce the field's invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~805: Add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the `<input>` element: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={...} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of the FAQ answer panel (has aria-controls) but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the panel is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2010: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons function as toggle buttons where one is always active, but they lack aria-pressed to communicate the active state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1110: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element inside the map function.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but the FormGroup wrapper is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2100: Change `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" error={...}>` to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={...}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button controls a submenu (has aria-controls and aria-haspopup) but does not have aria-expanded to indicate the submenu's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~940: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons indicate selection via CSS but lack aria-pressed to programmatically communicate which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1175: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the Monthly button. Line ~1185: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the Annual button.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles video playback state but lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether the video is currently playing or paused.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~970: Add `aria-pressed={isPlaying}` to the Button component.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides are visually hidden but remain accessible to screen readers because they lack aria-hidden="true". This causes users to encounter content that is not currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1210: Add `aria-hidden={!isVisible}` to the slide div: `<div role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={...} aria-hidden={!isVisible} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger button missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component renders a button to toggle a sub-menu, controlled by the isOpen state. However, the button lacks the aria-expanded attribute, so assistive technology users are not informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle component renders a button for 'Monthly' billing, controlled by the value state. It lacks aria-pressed, so screen reader users are not informed if this option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>

issuesFound.title: Form input missing aria-invalid implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying input element. When the form field is in an error state, screen readers are not notified of the invalidity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 469: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Form input references non-existent error message ID
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has an aria-describedby attribute pointing to 'newsletter-email-error' when in an error state. However, the parent FormGroup does not receive an errorId prop, so the error message element is never rendered, leaving the reference broken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
